rows.collision_note: (a) one word, two scopes: 'ghost_b' names BOTH the whole extraction stage (any engine writes ghost_b_extractions — including runpod_flash) AND specifically services/ghost_b.py's provider-LLM lane. The engine enum makes this concrete: engine='local' now means a private provider LLM running ghost_b.py's contract, while the old GLiNER/GLiREL sidecar is 'legacy_local' (extraction_contract.py:15-19) — the word 'local' silently changed meaning across the rename.
rows.evidence: worker.py:1923-1928 comments enumerate the engines; runpod_flash_extraction.py returns 'the same validated wire shape as ghost_b_local' (module docstring) and all engines persist to ghost_b_extractions.

rows.canonical: GLiNER (entity model family); distinguish plain GLiNER (local, entity-only) from GLiNER-ReLEX (RunPod, joint entity+relation)
rows.collision_note: (a) 'GLiNER' means two different models in two places: local lane = entity-only GLiNER + separate GLiREL for relations; RunPod lane = gliner-relex joint model doing both. P2.7b's 'GLiNER-ReLEX + spaCy windows... in the worker' is the remote stack; the local stack is a DIFFERENT composition producing the same artifact shape — this is exactly the parity surface P2.6/P2.7 must measure, not an accidental duplicate.
rows.evidence: ghost_b_local.py:9-16 (two GLiNER passes + GLiREL) vs runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:312,607 (single relex model, entity-only execution path note); test_runpod_flash_extraction.py:113 asserts output_mode=='joint_relex'.

rows.collision_note: (b) duplicate-implementation risk (intentional, must stay measured): relation extraction exists twice — GLiREL locally vs gliner-relex remotely (and a third time via provider-LLM RelationItems in ghost_b.py). GLiREL is NOT shipped in the RunPod worker (app.py deps list gliner+spacy only, app.py:538-539), so 'burst runs the full local stack remotely' (P2.7b) is currently false in the strict sense — the burst runs the RELEX stack, not the GLiNER+GLiREL stack.
rows.evidence: runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:538-546 pip deps contain gliner==0.2.27 and spacy but no glirel; ghost_b_local.py:262-263 imports gliner AND glirel for in-process mode.

rows.canonical: typed factual relations (RelationItem -> ghost_b_extractions.relations -> Neo4j edge -> lexicon factual_relations projection)
rows.collision_note: (a) one word, four layers (wire relation / durable artifact / graph edge / lexicon projection) — all the same concept flowing downstream, but line-item plan language like 'relation artifact' (P2.4) means the ghost_b_extractions row, not the Neo4j edge. Separately, P2.1 PLANS 'associated_concepts' (co-occurrence associations) and explicitly orders them kept DISTINCT from typed factual relations — associated_concepts has zero code today; do not implement it as another relations[] field.
rows.evidence: checklist P2.1 'Keep these associations distinct from typed factual graph relations'; grep associated_concepts/usage_frames/semantic_profile in backend = no hits.

per-relation check; mismatch -> warn-keep (domain_range_warn) or soft remap to related_to (domain_range_mismatch), original predicate preserved in source_predicate
existing per-batch violation-rate measurement (plan P2.5 bullet 3 partially built)
asserting coverage of remap/warn/keep paths

rows.collision_note: (b) HIGH duplicate-implementation risk: P2.5 reads as green-field ('build a reviewed compatibility table', 'annotate signature_valid') but the table and enforcement ALREADY exist under the domain_range name — and current behavior is STRONGER than the plan wants (it remaps/warns; P2.5 says annotate-only, no drop/remap). Implementing signature_valid beside _apply_domain_range would double the mechanism. Correct move: evolve DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP into the reviewed table and demote remap to annotation, one mechanism.
rows.evidence: ghost_b.py:2734-2760 remap path; P2.5 'Initially annotate signature_valid and violation reason; do not drop or remap edges automatically' — direct contradiction with the deployed remap-to-related_to behavior.

rows.canonical: Runpod Flash (serverless burst lane; two sub-lanes: runpod_flash extraction, runpod embed)
rows.collision_note: (a) 'RunPod' names two lanes with different contracts and different workers: extraction (runpod_flash_extractor/, polymath.runpod_gliner_relex.vN) and embedding (runpod_flash_embedder/, polymath.runpod_embed.v1, embedder.py mode='runpod'). NOT a duplicate implementation — the embed lane deliberately imports _AccountDispatcher and _submit_and_wait from runpod_flash_extraction.py (embedder.py:581), so routing/polling exists once. Keep it that way: any P2.7b dispatch changes land in runpod_flash_extraction.py and both lanes inherit.
rows.evidence: embedder.py:566-581 ('the enabled account with the least in-flight batches wins... services.runpod_flash_extraction._AccountDispatcher').

rows.canonical: Runpod burst dispatch (shipped: _AccountDispatcher + runpod_burst profile; planned: P2.7b orchestration layer)
rows.collision_note: (a) 'burst' is used for three unrelated things: extraction bursts (this row), embed bursts (embedder.py:8,62), and upload-admission bursts ('500-file simultaneous burst', ingestion/admission.py:12) plus incidental 'burst-load DNS blip' in llm.py. (b) build-vs-built split inside P2.7b itself: account routing/failover is DONE; disposition matrix, chunk-complete barrier, eligible-chunk manifest, per-burst chunks/sec + worker-seconds + cost-per-1k-chunks recording have NO code — grep 'barrier|manifest|disposition' in backend/services/ingestion returns nothing relevant. Nearest seam for the barrier: worker.py pass_plan/batch machinery (batches.py INGEST_PROFILES) which already sequences queryable-vs-enrichment passes.
rows.evidence: checklist P2.7b bullets vs code: only bullet 'dispatch routes request batches across enabled accounts' is checked [x]; batches.py:137 runpod_burst profile has no barrier semantics.

store_fields: PLANNED artifact: per-corpus classification reingest | re-extract-only | projection-only, owner-signed before any mass job; current known values live only in prose (checklist Active PoC Scope: v2=projection-only(frozen), mark+ecom=re-extract-only, UGO=canary)
rows.collision_note: No code symbol, no Mongo field. It is a GATE artifact, not a config: S3 (PLAN_CRITIQUE:82) produces it from the heading/OCR audit and it must precede S4's paid pass and S11's burst. Nearest existing seams: (1) per-corpus engine stamp resolved by resolve_extraction_contract (extraction_contract.py:164 — corpus-level extraction config already exists and is the natural home for a disposition field), (2) corpus strict-readiness (services/ingestion/readiness.py) which P2.7 already plans to wire extraction/concept readiness into. Risk (b): teams could encode disposition twice — once as a doc/table for sign-off and again ad hoc as engine toggles; make the corpus settings field the single source and render the sign-off table from it.
rows.evidence: grep -rni disposition backend --include=*.py = zero hits; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:82-83 and checklist P2.7b bullet 1 define it only in prose.
